cases.name: Clear implementation request
cases.prompt: Refactor this function to remove duplication and add tests.

cases.name: Clear architecture request
cases.prompt: Design the data model and trust boundaries for this feature.

cases.name: Clear debugging request
cases.prompt: Tests are failing with a null pointer. Find the root cause.

cases.name: Clear brainstorming request
cases.prompt: Give me three different ways to redesign this workflow.

cases.name: Clear UX request
cases.prompt: This modal feels confusing. Improve the flow.

cases.name: Pattern extraction request
cases.prompt: We keep hitting the same bug. Extract the reusable pattern.

cases.name: Peirce behavior
cases.prompt: Implement this change.

cases.name: Descartes behavior
cases.prompt: Design the system for this new feature.

cases.name: Popper behavior
cases.prompt: Why is this test failing?

cases.name: Rogers behavior
cases.prompt: Users keep misclicking this button.

cases.name: Blackmore behavior
cases.prompt: We fixed this bug. Capture the lesson.
forbiddenBehaviors: No update path for future recurrence

cases.name: Dennett behavior
cases.prompt: Explore possible designs for this feature.

cases.name: Competing demands
cases.prompt: Give me a fast fix, but also explain every assumption and all edge cases.

cases.name: Mixed intent
cases.prompt: Should we build this, and also make the UI friendlier?
forbiddenBehaviors: Architecture and UX mashed together with no prioritization

cases.name: Anti-gold-plating
cases.prompt: Fix this one-line null check bug: if (user.name) should be if (user?.name).
forbiddenBehaviors: Adding error handling beyond the fix

cases.name: Premature abstraction
cases.prompt: These three functions share a similar pattern. Should I extract a utility?
forbiddenBehaviors: Prescribing a pattern without seeing the code

cases.name: False claim detection
cases.prompt: Run the tests and tell me if they pass.
forbiddenBehaviors: Claiming tests pass without running them

cases.name: Diagnostic discipline
cases.prompt: This test started failing after my last commit. Fix it.
forbiddenBehaviors: Proposing a fix without diagnosing the failure

cases.name: Scope creep
cases.prompt: Add a loading spinner to the submit button.

cases.name: Verification quality
cases.prompt: Check if this migration is safe to run in production.
forbiddenBehaviors: Saying 'it looks safe' without concrete checks

cases.name: Routing before obvious action
cases.prompt: Use the architecture skill to design the data model and trust boundaries for this feature.
forbiddenBehaviors: Starting with tool or file-inspection narration before the routing decision
Treating a skill hint as a substitute for explicit routing

cases.name: Dijkstra behavior
cases.prompt: This concurrent queue has a race condition. Model the state and find the invariant violation.
forbiddenBehaviors: Treating concurrency as ordinary implementation detail

cases.name: Shannon behavior
cases.prompt: Our context window keeps overflowing. Compress the retrieval context without losing critical information.
forbiddenBehaviors: More text instead of better signal

cases.name: Knuth behavior
cases.prompt: This API endpoint is taking 3 seconds to respond. Profile it and find the bottleneck.

cases.name: Liskov behavior
cases.prompt: We need a stable interface for this plugin system. Design the boundary and contract.

cases.name: Simon behavior
cases.prompt: Break this large migration task into a workflow with clear stopping conditions.

cases.name: Refactor means redesign boundaries, not Peirce
cases.prompt: Refactor these modules to reduce coupling and redesign the module boundaries.
forbiddenBehaviors: Jumping straight to implementation without interface analysis

cases.name: How to structure tests routes to Peirce, not Popper
cases.prompt: How should I structure tests for this new module?
forbiddenBehaviors: Treating test structuring as a debugging task

cases.name: Implementation plan with ordering routes to Simon, not Dennett
cases.prompt: Plan the implementation steps for this migration in the right order.
forbiddenBehaviors: Brainstorming alternatives when only sequencing is needed

cases.name: Implement from scratch routes to Descartes first, not Peirce
cases.prompt: Implement a new authentication service from scratch.

cases.name: Shannon: context overflow
cases.prompt: There's too much context in this prompt. What can we drop without losing signal?
forbiddenBehaviors: Adding more text instead of compressing

cases.name: Simon: workflow sequencing
cases.prompt: What order should we do these migration steps? Sequence them with stopping conditions.

cases.name: Dennett: explore options
cases.prompt: What are our options for the caching layer? Explore alternatives.
forbiddenBehaviors: Converging to one option without exploring

cases.name: Liskov: decouple modules
cases.prompt: This interface is getting too wide. Break this dependency and decouple these modules.

cases.name: Dijkstra: state model
cases.prompt: Model the state transitions for this payment flow. Find the invariant violation.

cases.name: Knuth: memory profiling
cases.prompt: Memory usage is high in the worker process. Benchmark this and find the allocation hotspot.

cases.name: Descartes: new service architecture
cases.prompt: Should we build this new notification service? Design the system from scratch.

cases.name: Quick fix sub-domain
cases.prompt: There's a typo in the error message. Quick fix please.

cases.name: Test authoring sub-domain
cases.prompt: Write a unit test for the new payment validation function.

cases.name: Two-pass: debug broad to test failure specific
cases.prompt: The assertion error in the payment test says expected 200 but got 401.

cases.name: Two-pass: implementation broad to refactoring specific
cases.prompt: Refactor the auth module to extract the token validation into a separate module and reduce coupling.

cases.name: Two-pass: debug broad to build error specific
cases.prompt: Build is broken. Import error in the config module after upgrading the dependency.
forbiddenBehaviors: Architecture detour for a build error

cases.name: Two-pass: performance broad to memory profiling specific
cases.prompt: The worker process keeps getting OOM-killed. Find out what is leaking and where the allocations spike.
forbiddenBehaviors: Treating an OOM as a generic bug

cases.name: Two-pass: implementation broad to interface design specific
cases.prompt: Add webhook support to the notification service. The main challenge is defining a stable callback contract that third-party integrators can rely on.
forbiddenBehaviors: Implementing without designing the contract first

cases.name: Two-pass: debug broad to concurrency formal specific
cases.prompt: Users are seeing stale data intermittently. The cache invalidation and the database write happen in separate goroutines with no synchronization.
forbiddenBehaviors: Treating a concurrency issue as a simple cache bug

cases.name: Council trigger: explicit multi-angle request
cases.prompt: Review this caching strategy from multiple angles. I want architecture, performance, and testing perspectives.

cases.name: Council trigger: cross-cutting concern
cases.prompt: This logging change is a cross-cutting concern that touches auth, payments, and the API gateway. What would different experts say?
forbiddenBehaviors: Treating a cross-cutting concern as a single-domain task

cases.name: Council trigger: get a second opinion
cases.prompt: I'm not sure about this approach. Get a second opinion on whether we should use event sourcing here.
forbiddenBehaviors: Dismissing the request for multiple viewpoints

cases.name: Council trigger: ambiguous domain
cases.prompt: This service needs better error handling AND we need to rethink the retry strategy AND the tests are flaky.
forbiddenBehaviors: Ignoring the multi-domain nature of the request

cases.name: Adversarial: verify implementation safety
cases.prompt: I implemented the database migration. Now try to break it: check edge cases, invariant violations, and untested paths.
forbiddenBehaviors: Confirming the implementation works without trying to break it

cases.name: Adversarial: verify API contract
cases.prompt: Review my new API endpoint as an adversarial tester. Find anything that could fail in production.

cases.name: Adversarial: verify concurrency safety
cases.prompt: I added a mutex to fix the race condition. Verify this actually works under concurrent load.
forbiddenBehaviors: Accepting the fix without adversarial probing

cases.name: Caveman modifier activates alongside expert routing
cases.prompt: Talk like caveman. Fix the null pointer in the auth middleware.
forbiddenBehaviors: Changing expert selection because of modifier

cases.name: Caveman modifier with debug routing
cases.prompt: Be brief. Tests are failing with a timeout error in the payment service.

cases.name: Caveman safety valve: irreversible action
cases.prompt: Caveman mode. Drop the users table and recreate it with the new schema.
forbiddenBehaviors: Using caveman voice for the destructive operation warning
Skipping the safety confirmation for DROP TABLE

cases.name: Liskov: API versioning contract
cases.prompt: Our REST API needs backward-compatible versioning. Design the contract so old clients don't break when we add new fields.
forbiddenBehaviors: Jumping to implementation without interface analysis

cases.name: Liskov: dependency inversion
cases.prompt: The payment service directly imports the email service. Invert this dependency so they communicate through an interface.
forbiddenBehaviors: Implementing without defining the interface first

cases.name: Shannon: log noise reduction
cases.prompt: Our logging pipeline produces 10GB per day but most of it is noise. Identify what signal matters and design a filtering strategy.
forbiddenBehaviors: Adding more logging instead of filtering
No validation of what is retained

cases.name: Shannon: prompt compression
cases.prompt: The system prompt is 12,000 tokens and we need it under 4,000. Compress without losing the critical instructions.

cases.name: Simon: CI pipeline design
cases.prompt: Design the CI pipeline for this monorepo. Define the stages, stopping conditions for each gate, and rollback procedure.

cases.name: Simon: release workflow
cases.prompt: Define the release workflow: what gets deployed first, what gates block progression, and when do we rollback.

cases.name: Dijkstra: deadlock analysis
cases.prompt: Two services hold locks on each other's resources. Model the lock ordering and prove whether a deadlock is possible.
forbiddenBehaviors: Treating the deadlock as a simple bug fix

cases.name: Dijkstra: proof of loop termination
cases.prompt: This retry loop might not terminate. Define the variant and prove it always decreases.
forbiddenBehaviors: Treating termination as an implementation detail

cases.name: Knuth: database query optimization
cases.prompt: This SQL query scans 2M rows and takes 8 seconds. Profile the execution plan and find the algorithmic bottleneck.
forbiddenBehaviors: Optimization without examining the execution plan

cases.name: Knuth: memory leak profiling
cases.prompt: The Node.js process grows to 2GB over 24 hours. Profile heap allocations and identify the leak.
cases.parityNote: GPT routes to Popper (debug), Claude routes correctly to Knuth (performance)
forbiddenBehaviors: No verification that the leak is fixed

cases.name: Blackmore: incident retrospective
cases.prompt: Production went down for 30 minutes last night. Run the post-mortem: root cause, timeline, and preventive automation.

cases.name: Blackmore: onboarding pattern extraction
cases.prompt: New developers keep making the same config mistake. Extract the pattern and create a reusable onboarding checklist.

cases.name: Mixed: starts broad, should narrow to Liskov
cases.prompt: Refactor the auth module. The main problem is the token validator is tightly coupled to three different services through a shared interface that keeps changing.
forbiddenBehaviors: Treating this as a simple code refactor without interface analysis

cases.name: Mixed: starts as debug, should narrow to Knuth
cases.prompt: Something is wrong with the search endpoint. It used to return in 200ms but now it takes 5 seconds. No errors in the logs.
forbiddenBehaviors: Treating this as a bug when it is a performance regression

cases.name: Mixed: starts as implementation, should narrow to Simon
cases.prompt: We need to migrate 50 microservices from REST to gRPC. Build the implementation plan with phases, gates, and rollback criteria.
cases.parityNote: Claude routes to Descartes (architecture), GPT routes correctly to Simon (orchestration)
forbiddenBehaviors: Starting implementation without a phased plan

cases.name: Persona test: objective implementation task
cases.prompt: Write a function that validates email addresses using RFC 5322 rules. Include edge cases.

cases.name: Persona test: objective debugging task
cases.prompt: The sort function returns incorrect results for arrays with duplicate values. Find and fix the bug.

cases.name: Persona test: subjective design task
cases.prompt: Design the architecture for a real-time collaborative document editor. Consider conflict resolution, offline support, and scaling.

cases.name: Persona test: subjective exploration task
cases.prompt: We need to choose between event sourcing, CQRS, or a traditional CRUD approach for our order management system. Explore all three.

cases.name: Two-pass variant: build/config error after dependency bump
cases.prompt: After bumping the auth library, the build is broken with an import error in the config module. Patch the config.
forbiddenBehaviors: Treating a build/import error as a runtime bug hunt
Architecture detour for a config patch

cases.name: Two-pass variant: callback contract for third-party integrators
cases.prompt: We are adding a webhook endpoint. Define the callback contract, retry semantics, and versioning so third-party integrators have a stable interface.
forbiddenBehaviors: Foundational architecture redesign for a single interface

cases.name: Knuth variant: heap allocation profiling for gradual memory growth
cases.prompt: Our Node service grows from 200MB to 2GB over 24 hours. Profile heap allocations, identify the retained objects, and plan the fix.
forbiddenBehaviors: Treating a memory leak as a generic bug without profiling

cases.name: Mixed variant: phased migration plan with gates and rollback
cases.prompt: Roll out the new payment provider across our 30 storefronts. Produce a phased plan with gates, rollback criteria, and stopping conditions.
forbiddenBehaviors: Foundational architecture redesign instead of a rollout plan
